summary: PORTFOLIO MANAGER Iteration 1A corrective validation — backend only, live HTTP against EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. 66/66 tests PASS in 16.84s. Both defect areas confirmed fixed: (1) ALLOCATION OWNER INTEGRITY — POST/PUT /api/portfolio/resource-allocations now rejects with HTTP 400 for nonexistent owner_id (random uuid) and for foreign-user references across task/project/knowledge_journey; standalone still requires owner_id=null; on a rejecting PUT the persisted row remains unchanged (owner_type/owner_id/quantity intact). (2) MONEY AS DECIMAL/Decimal128 — financial_accounts.current_value, monthly_money_commitments.amount, and money resource_allocations.quantity round-trip as decimal STRINGS with exact precision preserved (e.g. "9999.9999", "1234.5678", "99.9500"). NaN/Infinity/-Infinity/negative all yield HTTP 400. Time-allocation quantity remains integer minutes serialised as string ("60", isinstance str). Money-position aggregate returns two-decimal-place strings; the exact iteration-1A scenario (bank 100.50 + cash 25.25 liquid USD; stock semi_liquid excluded; USD credit_card liability excluded; commitments income 500.25 fixed, expense 100.10 fixed, expense 50.05 flexible, saving 10.10 fixed, investment 5.20 fixed) yielded opening_liquid_assets="125.75", planned_income="500.25", fixed_outflows="100.10", flexible_outflows="50.05", planned_savings="10.10", planned_investments="5.20", available_for_flexible_spending="460.55". Empty currency returns all fields as "0.00". Regression suite green on /api/auth/me, /api/domains, /api/goals, /api/tasks, /api/checkins, /api/knowledge/journeys, plus every pre-existing portfolio validation rule (enums, HH:mm, YYYY-MM, YYYY-MM-DD, effective windows, duration=quantity for time, currency rules, user isolation across all 4 collections).
critical_code_review_comments: Owner integrity is validated by _validate_owner_reference in portfolio_manager.py: single indexed find_one({id, user_id}) per non-standalone allocation on POST, and again on PUT only when owner_type or owner_id are in the incoming payload — cheap and correct. Existence + ownership combined into one query prevents any information leak about foreign rows.

Money storage uses bson.decimal128.Decimal128 throughout: _parse_money_input strictly rejects bool, NaN, Infinity, and negative; strings/floats/ints all normalise through str(...) before Decimal, so no binary-float representation is ever persisted. _money_from_stored/_dec_to_str produce plain decimal notation (never scientific) and preserve exact caller precision.

Only *derived* aggregates in money-position are quantised to two decimals via _MONEY_OUTPUT_QUANTUM; individual stored values keep their input precision. Verified: 100.50+25.25=125.75 and 125.75+500.25-100.10-50.05-10.10-5.20=460.55, all serialised as strings.
PUT re-runs owner integrity only when owner_type/owner_id are present in the incoming payload; unchanged fields skip the DB round-trip. Correct.
Time-allocation quantity is stored as int and projected via str(int(q)) — response type is 'str' as required, while stored type stays int for correct duration validation.
Foreign-user rejection returns 400 (not 404). The message explicitly says 'no matching row exists for this user' — this is the correct security posture (no information disclosure about foreign IDs).
seed_data_creation: Each test module run signs up fresh TEST_pm_<uuid>@example.com users; all created rows are prefixed TEST_. Iteration-1A scenario creates 4 accounts + 5 commitments under a throwaway user; regression flow creates one TEST_regress_kj journey. No shared/production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live-HTTP portfolio suite is /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio_live.py — 66 tests, self-contained, uses only EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Run: `python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio_live.py -v`. Key contracts: (a) money values are decimal STRINGS in every response; (b) money-position aggregates are quantised to two decimals; (c) owner_type ∈ {task, project, knowledge_journey, standalone} — standalone REQUIRES owner_id=null; the other three require an owner_id that both exists and belongs to the caller (400 otherwise, with no information disclosure about foreign rows); (d) time-allocation quantity is integer minutes serialised as string; (e) NaN/Infinity/negative money values → 400.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects. Both corrective fixes verified end-to-end via live HTTP.
